categories.name: Opinions
categories.description: Strong views on how to build software. Most of these we will defend in a room of skeptical engineers.

posts.title: Why most LLM apps fail in production (and how to make yours boring)
posts.description: Free-form LLM output is a runtime bug surface, not a product. Here's why we treat Pydantic schemas as the contract, validate every call, and route failures to humans.

posts.title: Why we build with n8n instead of writing admin panels
posts.description: Most internal tools are forms wired to databases — exactly what n8n already is. Here's what we use n8n for, and the line where it stops being the right answer.

posts.title: Chat is the worst UI for an LLM
posts.description: Chat windows dump cognitive load on the user and reliability burden on the model. The highest-leverage LLM applications are pipelines, not conversations.

categories.name: AI Engineering
categories.description: How we build production LLM systems — schemas, evals, prompt versioning, cost control, and human-in-the-loop fallbacks.

categories.description: Lending and fintech engineering — KYC, bureau integrations, decisioning workflows, and operational patterns that hold up under audit.

categories.description: WhatsApp Business API, Meta Embedded Signup, webhooks, and the policy questions Meta actually asks during app review.
